title: ++CHRCH Access
body_html:
2. What Makes A Strong "Message"?

First... What IS a "message"?

A message is a story.

It starts like this:

"Once upon a time the world was one way and then someone came along and showed people a new way."

A better way.

That someone is you.

Your message takes "your people" (the actual heroes of the story) from their old conception of the world to a whole new one.

And YOU are the tour guide taking them to this new world.

The more that you:

1. tailor your story to your audience,

2. and the clearer the path is to how you’re getting them there

--the more powerful your message will be in uniting "your people" behind you.

For an artist, their message is their worldview. (A brand new way of seeing / doing things). It's your way of expressing your artistic vision expressed to your audience.

For a brand, a message describes your values and how your product (and way of doing business) backs up those values.

A strong message always speaks to a specific audience and always shows them a clear path to this new world.


A message is not a slogan.

“Fuck fear” is not a message.

“Freedom at all cost” is not a message.

"Be yourself" is not a message

At worst, they're faceless slogans.

At best, they're nice to put on a t-shirt maybe.

But a message... Those are the words that clearly show us your vision of a better world (or new way of thinking) and it tells us EXACTLY HOW YOU'RE GONNA HELP TAKE US THERE.

Compare this:

The Mystery School slogan:

"Make art not content."

To The Mystery School message behind it:

"A culture that worships image over substance is toxic. It’s up to artists and creatives to restore some sanity to the world by outsmarting the content makers and releasing ART drenched in authenticity and depth. I want to make creativity a new religion, by mentoring the next generation of artists who want to find and share their authentic voice. "


The 3 ingredients of a strong message:

1. Invites people to embrace a new ideal that hasn't materialized in the world yet (THE WHY).

2. It shows us how you plan on taking your audience there (THE HOW). Without "the how" you're only venting.

3. It is contrarian enough to have hardcore fans (your niche audience - YOUR WHO), AND haters. 


Do you know YOUR message?

If not, use this MESSAGE FORMULA to help you (replace what's in the brackets).

THE MESSAGE FORMULA:

The way that (situation / circumstance / group of people -- right now) is (variation of: outdated / boring / fucked). I'd like to (the better situation you imagine) by (doing this particular thing - THE HOW). (Your potential 'believers' - YOUR NICHE AUDIENCE), are you with me?


(humorous but real example: Kim Kardashian (when she was starting out): "I hate how society's beauty standard is all about being skinny and being "put together". I want to embrace my curves-- and have the freedom to be raw & messy just like real life is-- That's what my reality show is gonna be about. Thick, real girls getting ahead by showing the things that other people in Hollywood sweep under the rug. Who's with me?")

If you're having trouble finding your message... This whole section, 'The Book Of Purpose', will help you find it.

Complete and Continue

    The way this society I live in constantly drives people to seek external validation and comfort is making us burnt out, anxious, and lonely. I'd like to have a world where there is no right or wrong in selfgrowth, where we can accept the beauty of the unknown and people can be honest about their emotions and feelings. I aim to foster this honesty through my art form, which is multimedia performance art. Burnt-out generation and mental health seekers, are you with me?

            Message: "The way that women are staying in toxic relationships and shrinking their lives because they've been told that 'safe' misery is better than uncertain freedom is fucked. I'd like to show women what extraordinary lives look like when you choose to leave what confines you by combining deep liberation work with immersive art experiences that reconnect you to wonder, play, and the wild possibility of who you're meant to become. Women who are ready to stop surviving and start thriving - are you with me?" I'm still working through my own creative path. I use to do interact and immersive art but have been playing it safe making content for meta as a motion designer. I've been really drawn to photography. Last year I did 365 sunrise walks and took a photo each day and shared it. I feel like I'm still in transition creatively but this is what I'm starting with.

                Drew Peterson
                a year ago
                Reply
                Link
                At the risk of getting banned, I'm not an artist, I'm an engineer. Is the Eiffel Tower, engineering or art? perhaps both? Maybe I'm safe. An engineer takes existing things, and puts them together in a meaningful way, or intended purpose. My day job is computer systems for hospitals, but my off hours delight is Vacuum amp technology. I have built several guitar amps, and have played for about 35 years, not seriously. Reading this section makes me realize that it's not the guitar I want to be more artistic about, but engineering of all the components. I have read (well, partially) "Of Sound Mind: How Our Brain Constructs a Meaningful Sonic World" It deals with how are brain processes sound. I have experimented with acoustic advantages of different speaker cabinets and driver configurations. And of course, analog audio electronics. This course is part of this journey as well, although maybe not what was intended. But sometimes it works out that way, Cotton swabs were not intended for your ears, or at least they say that on the package... There are different aspects I like of this but mostly sharing what I created with other people. Which surprising few are even interested. Other things that I would consider inferior in sound or quality, I feel get much more undeserving praise. Yes, I'm jealous of course, but a commonality I've found is that they are presented in a more entertaining way. "Eye-Candy" if you will. Partially this is my fault, as I have gravitated to the engineering design aspects and not the easy of use, or aesthetics. Apple products seem to try and fit both, and I know there is a whole concept of industrial art where form and function can exist. And I must admit I'm getting tired of everything looking "Homemade" instead of "Handmade." I've enclosed a picture of a preamp I made. Modeled after a amp from the 60's, Not the enclosure but the circuit. It's intention is to be a "straight to a computer interface", PA system or powered speaker. It has an FX loop so pedals can be inserted in different parts of the "signal chain" and I think sounds great. I mean, it's ugly as sin, and not the "so ugly it's awesome", either. It's a prototype, and I wanted to dress it up bit. I'm stuck on what that should be, or even what a brand would be if I was to repackage, move controls and "doll it up" a bit. Maybe not just visually but with feel. Like a satisfying click when a dial returns to the 12 o'clock position. Different spacing of knobs. I don't want retro. Modern amps are often just the same rehashed look for the last 40 years. Perhaps I'm only venting, not describing. Ok, let me take a crack at the formula before continuing. The way that we make music at home is missing the tactile experience. I'd like to create a more hands on experience by making equipment that sounds good and has knobs and sliders that you can actually touch rather than solely a computer plugin, bridging the gap between new and old technologies. Independent Music Creators, are you with me? hmm....needs a little work.

                  Damn that got me to realise I’m really unclear on my message. There are 3 main themes, semi-messages that are super important to me. But I struggle with joining into one coherent message 1 Don’t abandon yourself - feel the feelings and realise you’re only the awareness of them. SIT WITH YOURSELF. 2 Ratio between consuming and creating will directly correlate to your life happiness. Aka create more consume less 3 Growing up in our culture synonymous to loosing the magic/wonder/playfulness we used to have. Bring back play into your life. Playing = pure presence. You do just because it’s fun, you don’t think about what to have for dinner, there’s no “goal” in it - it just is. Will proceed to book of purpose 🙏

                      This is my message BEFORE reading the Book of Purpose. I want to praise the work created by real people’s hands, developed over a long time with dedication, passion and discipline. I’ll do this by sharing my own art process using traditional media and encouraging others to do the same. Artists, are you with me? I don’t know if it’s okay to share my Instagram link in here, so if someone would like to connect with me, I’m @liacaldas almost everywhere ;-)

I want to show them the example (I'm 19) by creating Christian music. Young Christians of France, are you with me? What do you think?

                                  Here's my 'message' before going further, just for the record, as I haven't put it into words like this myself. The way that music is a means to transcend your own situation, transcend the expectations of what's possible in the minds of people, is not pushed to it's fullest, grinds my gears. The mundane is dizzying and it feels like there aren't enough yogis(spiritual practitioners in the indian/tibetean tradition) who come from a place of truth and divine intensity. Think kendrick lamar about to do a cultural reset, the weeknd showing the world what's possible for a reclusive chubby ethiopian canadian high school dropout, etc. I want to come with intensity, show the vast expanses of the brilliance a single person can encompass when pointed towards the higher plane of consciousness, and knock people out of their fucking senses and not be able to sleep that day they come across my work. Idk why but the fact that I got into music production was cus I heard the sound design in skrillex's scary monsters and nice sprites and I was just floored. And my personal quote that I've been saying for years is "impress is for peasants, I'm here to shock mfs". So I guess my message is to go all out, make this shit a reality and show the world how it's fucking done. Just set shit straight, no mediocrity, no meek intents, no 'practical' visions. Bro people meditate enough to be able to reincarnate in a specific time, place and shit(look into tibetean yogis/rinpoches). We should be able to achieve fuckin wayyyy more than that. Shit that'd make aliens think about wtf were they doing all that time.

                                      The music Dutch people listen to and their culture of compulsive normativity in music taste is stiffling, uninspiring and unimaginative. The dominant music industry in The Netherlands is complicit in keeping spiritual, sonically adventurous music outside of mainstream radio. I would like to reinvigorate Dutch music by enriching it with 1) traditions from outside the dominant cultural forms of music in other countries, and 2) inject music with an adventurous sensibility. Ideally my audience is regular Dutch people who don't listen to a lot of music, and avid Dutch music listeners. I think making Dutch music not suck ass is one of my motivations amongside others, namely creating accessible, beautiful, dreamy pop music with attention to detail.

                                                    2 years ago

                                                    I love this! Thank you. This unlocked something for me. Can a message be too big? Can it change? The way that the lower classes have been used, abused and denied basic rights is wrong. For too long, we have been conditioned by the oppressive system to be okay with our current status in life. Instead, we are led to dream of a nearly impossible ideal life while we blame ourselves for any suffering we experience when we face anxiety about food, shelter, health. Rather than pitting against each other, we need to come together to change, abolish the systems. or work outside of the systems that hold us down. I'd like to redefine our expectations for basic human rights by demystifying the role of capitalism and the fascism lurking within it. At the same time, I love to imagine ways of living that are intrinsically connected with nature with regenerative food and plenty of wonderful places to cook, eat, and sleep comfortably.
                                                    Ashris Choudhury
